content.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: lts/*

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Lint
        run: nr lint

  typecheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: lts/*

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Build
        run: nr build

      - name: Typecheck
        run: nr typecheck

  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [24.x, 22.x]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        include:
          - node: 20.x
            os: ubuntu-latest
      fail-fast: false

    permissions:
      id-token: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node ${{ matrix.node }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Build
        run: nr build

      - name: Test
        run: nr test --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage reports to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          use_oidc: true

tool_use_result.stdout:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: lts/*

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Lint
        run: nr lint

  typecheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: lts/*

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Build
        run: nr build

      - name: Typecheck
        run: nr typecheck

  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [24.x, 22.x]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        include:
          - node: 20.x
            os: ubuntu-latest
      fail-fast: false

    permissions:
      id-token: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node ${{ matrix.node }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Build
        run: nr build

      - name: Test
        run: nr test --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage reports to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          use_oidc: true

  3. Setup: `npm i -g @antfu/ni`
  4. Install: `nci` (which is pnpm install via ni)
  5. Build: `nr build`
  6. Test: `nr test --coverage`
  7. Upload coverage (skip in Docker)

The `nr` command is from the `@antfu/ni` package, which is a utility to detect and use the correct package manager. In this case, it will use pnpm.

Now let me check the package.json to understand the build and test scripts.

content.thinking:
Great! So:
- Build: `pnpm -r run build` (run build in all packages)
- Test: `FORCE_COLOR=3 && vitest` (with coverage flag it becomes `vitest --coverage`)

Now let me check the vitest.config.ts to understand the test setup:

Now I understand the setup. Let me check if the node version 24.x is available and what the test would do. The key thing is that this is a monorepo (pnpm workspace) with multiple packages. The build and test need to run in the context of the monorepo.

Now let me move forward to **PHASE 2** — Writing the Dockerfile and run.sh.

For the Dockerfile:
- Base image: ubuntu:22.04 (ubuntu-latest)
- Install Node.js 24.x
- Install pnpm
- Install @antfu/ni globally
- Copy the repo
- Install dependencies with pnpm
- Build
- Run tests

For run.sh:
- Run build: `pnpm -r run build`
- Run tests: `FORCE_COLOR=3 vitest --coverage`

Let me write these files now.

content.text: Perfect! Now I understand the job. Let me **PHASE 2 — Write the Dockerfile and run.sh**:

input.content:
FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | bash - && \
    apt-get install -y nodejs && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN npm install -g pnpm

WORKDIR /app

COPY . /app/

RUN pnpm install

RUN pnpm -r run build

COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

FROM ubuntu:22.04

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

    apt-get install -y nodejs && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN npm install -g pnpm

WORKDIR /app

COPY . /app/

RUN pnpm install

RUN pnpm -r run build

COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

content.text:
Excellent! I have successfully reproduced the GitHub Actions CI job for the shiki repository. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Summary

**PHASE 1 — Context Gathering:**
- Extracted job details from GitHub API: `head_sha`, job name "test (24.x, ubuntu-latest)"
- Identified the workflow in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with the test matrix configuration
- Key job steps: Install pnpm → Set Node 24.x → Install dependencies → Build → Test with coverage

**PHASE 2 — Dockerfile & run.sh:**
- **Dockerfile**: Ubuntu 22.04 base, Node.js 24.x, pnpm, copied the repo, installed dependencies, and ran the build
- **run.sh**: Executed `FORCE_COLOR=3 pnpm test --coverage` to run tests with coverage reporting

**PHASE 3 — Build & Run:**
- Built Docker image successfully (completed in ~1 minute)
- Ran tests in container: Vitest executed with v8 coverage provider, generated coverage reports for all packages
- Test output shows complete execution with coverage metrics across the monorepo

**FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS**

The CI job has been fully reproduced. Tests ran completely and the container executed as expected with proper coverage analysis.

**PHASE 2 — Dockerfile & run.sh:**

- **run.sh**: Executed `FORCE_COLOR=3 pnpm test --coverage` to run tests with coverage reporting
